profile.name: Sergio Carvajal Appelgren
profile.title: Network & Systems Administrator | Infrastructure Specialist
profile.about: Network and Systems Administrator with 10+ years of experience designing, deploying, and optimizing secure enterprise IT infrastructures. Specialized in enterprise networking, Active Directory management, virtualization, and infrastructure modernization. Proven track record maintaining 98% network uptime across 2,000+ users in multi-site education and healthcare environments. Currently completing IT Infrastructure studies at Conestoga College while consulting on complex infrastructure projects.

experience.description: Designing and deploying secure network infrastructures for multiple clients across diverse environments.
highlights: Designed and deployed secure network infrastructures for multiple clients
Configured routing, VPNs, firewalls, wireless networks, and VLAN segmentation
Administered Linux servers (Debian/Ubuntu) with system hardening
Implemented and managed virtualized environments using Proxmox VE
Delivered Tier-2 technical support for networking and infrastructure
Provided infrastructure documentation and technical consulting

blog.title: Best Practices for Network Segmentation
blog.excerpt: Exploring VLAN segmentation strategies to improve network security and performance in enterprise environments.
blog.content: Network segmentation is critical for enterprise security. VLANs allow you to logically separate traffic, enforce security policies, and improve performance. Key practices include: isolating guest networks, separating IoT devices, protecting sensitive data areas, and implementing inter-VLAN routing with firewalls. Proper segmentation reduces attack surface and simplifies troubleshooting.

blog.title: Active Directory: Security Hardening Checklist
blog.excerpt: Essential security configurations for protecting your Active Directory infrastructure from common threats.
blog.content: Active Directory is the heart of Windows enterprise infrastructure. Proper hardening is essential. Key recommendations: enforce strong password policies, implement MFA for privileged accounts, restrict Domain Admin group membership, enable audit logging, implement Kerberos hardening, keep AD servers patched, and regularly review access permissions. Consider implementing Entra ID for hybrid security.

blog.title: Disaster Recovery Planning: A Practical Guide
blog.excerpt: Implementing effective backup and recovery procedures to ensure business continuity.
blog.content: Disaster recovery requires proper planning and testing. Start with a comprehensive backup strategy: identify critical systems, implement 3-2-1 backup rule (3 copies, 2 different media, 1 offsite), test recovery procedures regularly, document everything, and establish clear RPO/RTO objectives. Automated backups reduce human error. Cloud backup solutions provide geographic redundancy.

blog.title: VPN Configuration for Remote Workforce
blog.excerpt: Setting up secure site-to-site and remote access VPN for modern distributed teams.
blog.content: Modern workforce requires secure VPN solutions. Site-to-site VPNs connect branch offices securely. Remote access VPNs support work-from-home scenarios. Key considerations: choose appropriate encryption (IPSec vs OpenVPN), implement MFA, monitor VPN logs, optimize bandwidth, and test failover scenarios. Consider VPN appliances with threat prevention capabilities.
